definition: Commitment to honesty and ethical standards in all academic work
example: Academic integrity means doing your own work and giving credit to sources.

definition: The annual period during which students attend school, usually divided into terms
example: The academic year runs from June to April in most Indian schools.

definition: The process or fact of entering or being allowed to enter a place or organization.
example: Admission to the school requires passing an entrance examination.

definition: A task or piece of work assigned to someone as part of a job or course of study.
example: The assignment required students to interview a local community member.

definition: The action or state of going regularly to or being present at a place or event.
example: Students must maintain 75% attendance to sit for the final examination.

definition: A reference to a source of information used in a student's work
example: Every fact in the research paper required a proper citation.

definition: Techniques used by teachers to maintain an orderly and productive learning environment
example: Effective classroom management ensures all students can focus on learning.

definition: The final date by which a student must submit a task or assignment
example: The deadline for submitting the history project is next Friday.

definition: A disciplinary measure requiring a student to stay after school
example: The student received detention for repeatedly submitting homework late.

definition: The act of officially registering a student at a school or course
example: Enrolment for the new academic year begins in March.

definition: Activities outside the regular academic curriculum, such as sports and clubs
example: Participating in extracurricular activities helps build leadership skills.

definition: The teaching staff of a university or college.
example: The entire faculty attended the professional development workshop.

definition: Schoolwork that a student is required to do at home.
example: The homework for tonight is to read pages 45 to 60 of the novel.

definition: An experienced and trusted adviser.
example: Her mentor helped her develop stronger essay-writing skills.

definition: The practice of taking someone else's work or ideas and passing them off as one's own.
example: Plagiarism is taken seriously and can result in disqualification from the examination.

definition: The most important senior researcher or instructor, or the head of a school.
example: The principal announced a new English reading programme for all grades.

definition: Extra lessons provided to students who need additional support
example: The school arranged remedial classes for students struggling with mathematics.

definition: A half-year period in an academic year, typically 15 to 18 weeks
example: The course is divided into two semesters with exams at the end of each.
Late submission of the project would result in a ten percent penalty.

definition: Either a single number or variable, or numbers and variables multiplied together.
example: Results for the first term will be announced next week.

definition: A schedule showing the times and subjects of classes each day
example: The new timetable showed that science was moved to Monday mornings.

definition: A person who gives additional private or small-group teaching to students
example: The student hired a tutor to help with preparation for the board exams.
